Winter Park restaurant health grades — FAQ

How are Winter Park restaurant health grades calculated?

Florida issues no official grades, so we calculate an A–F score from each restaurant's most recent DBPR inspection — starting at 100 points and deducting for high-priority, intermediate, and basic violations. Of the 69 Winter Park restaurants we grade, 53 currently hold an A.

Does Florida assign letter grades to Winter Park restaurants?

No. The Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR) inspects restaurants and records violations but does not grade them. The grades shown here are calculated by us from those public inspection records.

How many restaurants are graded in Winter Park?

We currently publish 69 graded restaurants in Winter Park, with an average diner rating of 4.4 stars; 53 hold an A health grade.

See the full scoring method on our methodology page.

Central Florida Restaurants

Restaurant health inspection grades, dietary options, and diner ratings across Central Florida. Inspection data from Florida DBPR public records.
